studyGuide.title: Introduction
studyGuide.content: Arithmetic Progression (A.P.) is a simple list of numbers where the gap between any two numbers next to each other is always the same.

studyGuide.title: What is an A.P.?
studyGuide.content:
An A.P. is a sequence with a constant difference.
Example: 2, 5, 8, 11
Here, the gap is always 3. (5-2=3, 8-5=3)

studyGuide.title: General Form
studyGuide.content:
a, a + d, a + 2d, a + 3d, ...
Where:
a = first term
d = common difference

studyGuide.title: Important Formulas
studyGuide.content:
To find any position (nth term):
aₙ = a + (n − 1)d

To find the total sum:
Sₙ = n/2 [2a + (n − 1)d]

studyGuide.title: Important Points
studyGuide.content:
• The difference 'd' can be positive, negative, or zero
• Always subtract the old term from the new term to find 'd'
• We use A.P. in real life to track salaries or regular bank savings
